title: Saying Sorry
lesson: When you hurt someone, saying sorry helps make things better.
prompts: In a cosy sunny living room with a soft rug, warm afternoon light, the {INPUT_1_AGE} person from the input image stands on the far left playing happily with colourful wooden blocks, facing the camera with their face fully visible and a cheerful smile. On the right, {GENDER_M_AGE_06_RACE_WHITE} kneels beside a tall, neatly stacked block tower, looking proud. Eye-level medium-wide shot, photorealistic, cinematic photography style. Preserve the facial features, skin tone and hairstyle of the person from the input image.

In a cosy sunny living room with a soft rug, warm afternoon light, the {INPUT_1_AGE} person from the input image is on the far left, mid-step and reaching across, head turned toward the camera so the face stays clearly visible, with a surprised expression as their arm bumps a tall block tower. On the right, {GENDER_M_AGE_06_RACE_WHITE} sits on the rug, both hands flying up in dismay, watching the colourful wooden blocks tumble and scatter across the floor. Eye-level medium-wide shot, photorealistic, cinematic photography style. Preserve the facial features, skin tone and hairstyle of the person from the input image.

In a cosy sunny living room with a soft rug, warm afternoon light, colourful wooden blocks scattered across the rug, the {INPUT_1_AGE} person from the input image stands on the far left, wringing their hands uncertainly in front of them, facing the camera with their face fully visible and a worried expression. On the right, {GENDER_M_AGE_06_RACE_WHITE} sits among the fallen blocks hugging his knees, with a downturned, sad mouth. Eye-level medium shot, photorealistic, cinematic photography style. Preserve the facial features, skin tone and hairstyle of the person from the input image.

In a cosy sunny living room with a soft rug, warm afternoon light, the {INPUT_1_AGE} person from the input image stands alone on the far left, facing the camera with their face fully visible, one hand on their chest and a thoughtful, sorry expression as they hesitate. Scattered colourful wooden blocks lie on the rug in the foreground. Eye-level medium shot, photorealistic, cinematic photography style. Preserve the facial features, skin tone and hairstyle of the person from the input image.

In a cosy sunny living room with a soft rug, warm afternoon light, the {INPUT_1_AGE} person from the input image is on the far left, kneeling on the rug but with the head turned toward the camera so the face stays clearly visible, gently helping to pick up the colourful wooden blocks and rebuild the tower with a kind, apologetic smile. On the right, {GENDER_M_AGE_06_RACE_WHITE} kneels too, reaching for a block and beginning to smile. Eye-level medium-wide shot, photorealistic, cinematic photography style. Preserve the facial features, skin tone and hairstyle of the person from the input image.

In a cosy sunny living room with a soft rug, bright cheerful afternoon light, the colourful wooden block tower rebuilt tall behind them, the {INPUT_1_AGE} person from the input image is on the far left, facing the camera with their face fully visible and a happy, warm smile, sharing a friendly hug. On the right, {GENDER_M_AGE_06_RACE_WHITE} hugs back, beaming with joy. Eye-level medium shot, photorealistic, cinematic photography style. Preserve the facial features, skin tone and hairstyle of the person from the input image.
gists: In a cosy sunny living room, the child (far left) plays happily with wooden blocks while a young boy at right kneels proudly by a tall block tower he built.

In the living room, the child (far left), mid-step and reaching across with a surprised look, accidentally bumps the tall tower so the blocks tumble and scatter; the boy at right throws his hands up as he watches them fall.
In the living room with blocks scattered on the rug, the child (far left) wrings their hands, worried, while the sad boy at right sits among the fallen blocks — the child is unsure what to do.
In the living room with scattered blocks, the child stands alone with a hand on their chest and a thoughtful, sorry expression, hesitating to apologise.
In the living room, the child (far left) kneels helping rebuild the tower with an apologetic smile while the boy at right kneels too, starting to smile — the child says sorry and helps fix it.
In the living room with the tower rebuilt tall behind them, the child (far left) and the boy at right share a happy hug — saying sorry has mended the friendship.
texts: You were playing blocks with your friend. He had built a tall, wobbly tower. "Look how high!" he said.
As you reached across — crash! Your arm bumped the tower and all the blocks tumbled down.
Your friend looked at the fallen blocks and felt sad. You weren't sure what to do.
You took a deep breath. It was hard to say, but you knew the right thing to do.
"I'm sorry," you said, and you helped build the tower again. Your friend started to smile.
Soon you were laughing and hugging. Saying sorry had made everything better.
